provenance.source_corpus: Roadside revegetation with native plants: Experimental seeding and transplanting of stem cuttings

source_spans.text: The stratification treatment significantly enhanced seed germination of K. angustifolia, I. versicolor, J. effusus and E. vaginatum

nodes.name: Seed germination of K. angustifolia, I. versicolor, J. effusus and E. vaginatum

source_spans.text: The stratification treatment significantly enhanced seed germination of K. angustifolia, I. versicolor, J. effusus and E. vaginatum
germination was increased by 20% in organic matter mulch compared to the control
Hay-mat mulch and organic matter mulch provided higher survival of E. nigrum stem cuttings
Total biomass of I. versicolor was lower in hay-mat mulch and organic matter mulch than control
Root and shoot dry mass of the species was less in organic matter mulch than in hay-mat mulch and control
their shoot biomass was lower in hay-mat mulch than organic matter mulch
germination was increased by 20% in organic matter mulch compared to the control
Germination of I. versicolor was lower (5%) in hay-mat mulch than control
for J. communis the hay-mat mulch provided significantly more survival
Shoot length of E. nigrum cuttings was almost double in hay-mat mulch than in organic matter mulch
Total biomass of I. versicolor was lower in hay-mat mulch and organic matter mulch than control
The root-shoot ratio of E. nigrum was higher in hay-mat mulch than control and organic matter mulch
it caused significant reduction in shoot dry mass compared to organic matter mulch and control
Germination of I. versicolor was lower (5%) in hay-mat mulch than control
Hay-mat mulch and organic matter mulch provided higher survival of E. nigrum stem cuttings
